summary: Tested Input Validation & Moderation: backend sanitization, max-length truncation, and chat rate limiting (10/30s). 7/9 backend tests passed. Frontend maxLength attributes and chat char counter verified. One real backend bug found: POST /api/messages (direct messages) is missing sanitize_text() so HTML is not stripped and 2000-char limit is not enforced.

critical.issue: Direct/private message endpoint does NOT call sanitize_text(). HTML/<script> tags are stored as-is and content can exceed the 2000-char limit (verified storing 2500 chars). Per the requirements, direct messages must strip HTML and truncate to 2000.

passed_tests: POST /api/chat strips <b>/<i> HTML tags (clean text returned)
POST /api/chat with 1050 chars truncates response to 1000
PUT /api/chat/{id}/edit strips HTML and script tags on edit
POST /api/courses with 250-char title truncates to 200; description HTML stripped while preserving text
Chat rate limit: first 10 messages return 200, 11th returns 429 within a 30s window
Frontend chat input has maxLength=1000 (verified live)
Frontend chat character counter (chat-char-count) shows '850/1000' when typing 850 chars (>800 trigger working)
Frontend code review: Messages.jsx textarea maxLength=2000 + message-char-count counter at >1600
Frontend code review: CourseWizard.jsx title maxLength=200, description maxLength=2000
Frontend code review: LessonWizard.jsx title maxLength=200, description maxLength=2000
action_items: Fix POST /api/messages in /app/backend/routes/social.py send_private_message(): apply sanitize_text(data.content, LIMITS['direct_message']) before insert (mirror what create_comment / send_chat_message already do). Also reject empty content after sanitization.

(Optional consistency) Sanitization wasn't explicitly validated for groups create/rename, prompt replies, or auth update-name in this run. Files of reference indicate they're applied; main agent may want a targeted retest on /api/groups, /api/prompts/{id}/replies, and /api/auth/update-name.
critical_code_review_comments: social.py send_private_message (lines 239-259) constructs message with raw data.content - missing sanitize_text() call. Inconsistent with send_chat_message (line 81) and create_comment (line 27). This is the only confirmed gap in the announced feature.

rate_limit.py: chat_rate_limiter is a process-local in-memory singleton. Acceptable for single-worker deployments but will misbehave under multi-worker uvicorn or horizontal scaling. Consider Redis-backed limiter for production.

sanitize.py strip_html_tags uses single-pass regex; nested malformed tags like '<<script>script>' would still be partially handled but is a known limitation. For the given requirements (strip tags, decode entities, truncate) implementation is correct.
websocket.py applies both sanitize_text and chat_rate_limiter on incoming WS 'message' events (lines 151-156) - correctly mirrors HTTP /api/chat.
Frontend maxLength on inputs is a UX guard only; backend truncation is the source of truth - which is correctly implemented for chat/comments/courses/lessons.

success_rate.backend: 78% (7/9 pytest cases - both failures are the same /api/messages sanitize miss)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on items verified (maxLength + char counter on chat; code-level confirmation for Messages/CourseWizard/LessonWizard)

seed_data_creation: Tests create transient chat/comment/private-message rows using admin account; one course is created and immediately deleted in TestCourseSanitization. Note: many short chat messages produced during rate-limit and sanitize tests remain in db.chat_messages.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_input_validation_moderation.py after main agent applies sanitize_text + LIMITS['direct_message'] in send_private_message. The rate-limit test sleeps 31s twice (~62s) before chat operations to clear the in-memory window; do not run concurrent chat tests against the same admin within 30s. WebSocket sanitize/rate-limit was verified by code review only (live WS test not executed).
rca of the issue: Root cause: send_private_message in /app/backend/routes/social.py was not updated when input-validation feature was rolled out. The function inserts data.content directly into MongoDB. Reproduction: POST /api/messages with {teacher_id, content:'x'+'B'*2500} -> response.content contains raw HTML and length 2500. Fix: at the top of the handler, after the empty-content check, call data.content = sanitize_text(data.content, LIMITS['direct_message']) and then re-check for empty after sanitization (HTTPException 400).
